content:
# Mineralogy and Crystallography

**Summary**: Broad research theme addressed in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] contributed to the theme of Mineralogy

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] analyzed the mineralogical characteristics of PM using XRD.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] used mineralogy as a key volumetric property for seismic interpretation.

- [[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]] investigated mineralogy to determine sand purity and suitability for energy applications.

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] investigated the mineralogical variability from source to sink.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] used mineralogy to calculate average bulk and shear moduli.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] determined mineralogy to identify different granitoid suites.

- [[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025]] used mineralogy as a focus for automated identification.

- [[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025]] used mineralogy analysis to characterize basaltic rocks for potential carbon mineralization.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] focused on the mineralogy of magnesite and associated carbonate rocks.

## Key Subtopics



- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] analyzed the mineralogical characteristics of PM using XRD.

- [[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020]] used mineralogy as a key volumetric property for seismic interpretation.

- [[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]] investigated mineralogy to determine sand purity and suitability for energy applications.

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] investigated the mineralogical variability from source to sink.

- [[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023]] used mineralogy to calculate average bulk and shear moduli.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] determined mineralogy to identify different granitoid suites.

- [[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025]] used mineralogy as a focus for automated identification.

- [[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025]] used mineralogy analysis to characterize basaltic rocks for potential carbon mineralization.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] focused on the mineralogy of magnesite and associated carbonate rocks.

## Methods and Approaches Used



## Key Findings Across Sources

- contributed to the theme of Mineralogy ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]])

- calcite and montmorillonite clay minerals are the main constituents of the PM. ([[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016, p. 15]])

- Mineralogy is used to determine the rock's stiffness and integrity. ([[2020-almumtin|Almumtin, 2020, p. 19]])

- Pure quartzose sands (≥99% quartz) show superior thermo-physical properties. ([[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022, p. 15]])

- Samples were dominated by plagioclase feldspars (albite and anorthite) followed by pyroxenes. ([[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022, p. 15]])

- Used in Hill's averages for multi-mineral matrices. ([[2023-alsahwan|AlSahwan, 2023, p. 27]])

- Granitoids mainly consist of quartz (15-42%) and K-Feldspar (22-58%). ([[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024, p. 50]])

- SSL is poised to advance the field of mineralogy in petrography. ([[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025, p. 76]])

- Basalt is highly reactive with CO2-rich fluids due to its high content of divalent cations (Ca2+, Mg2+, Fe2+). ([[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025, p. 22]])

- Magnesite was identified as a high-purity mineral in 37 out of 52 samples via XRD. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 80]])
